D10 Imaging L4 Analyze Reference

Bokeh & Aperture Blades

an out-of-focus highlight is a tiny picture of the aperture itself — a five-bladed iris paints pentagons, a fifteen-bladed iris paints discs

A point of light that lies far from the focal plane spreads across the sensor in the shape of the aperture opening. With many curved blades the iris looks like a circle and bokeh balls come out smooth and round; with few straight blades the iris is a polygon and every highlight is the same polygon.

Drag the blade count and watch the iris on the left and the bokeh highlights on the right take the same shape. Wide stop-down — the iris polygon shows through every out-of-focus spot in the picture.
